task_completion.description: Open the 'T1 Head' MRI sample stack in ImageJ/Fiji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji window displaying the 'T1 Head' image stack, identifiable by the window title and the sagittal brain view (256x256 pixels, 129 slices).

task_completion.description: Segment the brain tissue from the background, CSF, and skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ Threshold tool is open and applied to the image, or the image stack is shown as a binary mask where the brain tissue is highlighted/isolated from the skull and background.

task_completion.description: Measure the tissue area across the slices of the stack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Results' window is visible, populated with multiple rows of area measurements corresponding to different slices of the stack.

task_completion.description: Calculate the total volume estimate based on the slice areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculator, script, spreadsheet, or terminal output showing the summation or volumetric calculation of the measured slice areas to produce a total volume.

task_completion.description: Save the per-slice area measurements and total volume estimate to the specified CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal (e.g., using 'cat'), or spreadsheet showing the contents of '~/ImageJ_Data/results/mri_volume_results.csv'. The file must contain a slice identifier column, the measured area per slice, and the total volume value.

integrity.description: The agent actually used ImageJ/Fiji to process the image stack.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ImageJ/Fiji GUI being actively used (e.g., menus, threshold dialogs, results tables) or an ImageJ macro being executed, rather than just a Python script bypassing the application entirely.

integrity.description: The measured areas reflect genuine anatomical variation across the MRI slices.
integrity.visual_evidence: The area values in the results table or CSV vary significantly across slices (small at the extremes, larger in the middle), rather than being constant or randomly generated numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a CSV with fabricated data without performing the measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear logical progression from opening the image, thresholding, generating the ImageJ results table, and saving those specific results to the final CSV.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be located at '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/mri_volume_results.csv'. The T1 Head sample is a 256x256x129 stack accessed via File > Open Samples > T1 Head. The CSV should contain at least 20 rows of slice measurements. Because it is a real brain scan, the area measurements must vary significantly across slices (standard deviation > 100), with the maximum area slice containing between 5,000 and 65,536 pixels.
